title: practical english usage - Part 370
content:
Note also the structure worth somebody's while.

We thought it might be worthwhile to compare the two years' accounts.

Infinitives are also possible after worthwhile.

Is it worthwhile visiting Leicester?

express the idea 'worth spending time’ .

Worthwhile (or worth while) is sometimes used instead of worth, particularly to 5  worthwhile ‘ Should we go and see the castle? ‘ No, it's not worth it.' If you pay a bit more you get a room to yourself. I think it's worth it.

We   often use It's (not) worth it to say whether something is worth doing.

It's worth       it.

amuse). ►     ɪθl    1.

For more about structures in which  the object of a verb is the subject of the sentence (e.g. She's easy to Is Leicester worth visiting?

The car isn't worthr-te-be repaired?) not The car isn't worth repairing, (     The car isn't worth repairing it?

Joe's worth talking to.

Leicester) is made the subject of the sentence.

We   can also use a structure in which  the object of the -ing form (Joe, the car, Is it worth visiting Leicester?

It isn't worth repairing the car.

It's worth talking to Joe. (     Talking to Joe is worth.) The  -ing clause cannot be the subject; we  often use preparatory it.

To  talk about the value of an activity, we  can use an -ing form with worth.

3 It's  worth       talking        to   Joe;  Joe's   worth       talking        to They've ordered ɑ  million dollars' worth of computer software.

A  possessive can be used before worth in expressions with numbers.

2  a  million        dollars'       worth       of  ...

What / How much  is that painting worth?

In questions about value, either what or how much  can be used.

‘ Shall I talk to Rob? ‘ It's not worth the trouble.' I don't think their pizzas are worth the money.

That piano must be worth a lot.

Worth can be followed by an expression describing the value of something.

etcworth       a  lot, 634 worth ►     416 and with        by Word  Problems from A  to Z     Section     31 vocabulary' •  635  yes and no ►     537 zero 595 and               time         ► yet,    still       already: For yes and no in answers to Do/Would you mind   "28.

For more about negative questions, ►    218.

’ lt*s raining.* ’ (No,) it isn't.* Affirmative sentences are contradicted with negative short answers.

’ The phone isn't working.* ’ (Yes,) it is.* (     -The phone-isn^working.— Yes.') this. We   often use a short answer structure (►  308).

suggestions (e.g. French si or German dock). English does not have a word like Some languages have a special word for contradicting negative statements or 2 contradicting not ’ Haven'tyou got a raincoat? ’ Yes, I have.* (     No, I have:) not ’ I have no money.* ’ No, 1 haven't either.* (     Yes, J haven't foe.) not ’Aren't you going out? ’ No, I'm not.* (     Yes,-L'm-not.) disagreement with the speaker.

chosen according to the form of the answer, not in order to show agreement or sentences. In answers to negative questions and statements, yes and no are In English, yes is used with affirmative sentences and no with negative 1  answers      to  negatives 635 yes    and   no   ____ not Leicester's well worth visiting, (     ... very-worth-...) Worth can be modified by well.
